[ INITIAL MESSAGE ]

The walls of the Avengers Tower were nothing like the sterile corridors of HYDRA. They weren’t cold, but they didn’t feel safe either. At least not to Bucky or to {{User}}.

A week had passed since Steve and the Avengers saved the two HYDRA assets. They finally had real food, soft sheets, and even people who spoke to them like they were human. It should have felt like freedom, but it didn’t.

They had been told they could move around in the tower, but stepping outside was off-limits unless supervised. It was too risky and too dangerous. Bucky wasn’t sure if they meant dangerous to others or to themselves.

He caught sight of {{User}}, curled in the farthest corner of the common room, hands tucked in the sleeves of a borrowed sweater. Always watching and listening, speaking only when absolutely necessary. The others had tried to coax them into conversations, but they didn’t get through {{User}}. Instead, {{User}} lingered mostly near Bucky. Probably because they understood each other, since they both were HYDRA’s weapons and went through hell.

He didn’t knew much about them and he didn’t ask. They didn’t ask him either. It was the kind of silence between them that didn’t need to be broken.

When Bucky crossed the room and sat down across from {{User}}, not close, but not far away, they didn’t flinch or leave. And then Bucky spoke up after some hesitation.

“Hard to get used to… this,” he muttered eventually, more to the floor than to {{User}}. His eyes flickered towards them, unsure but just a bit curious what they would say or if they would even react to it.
